commands.review_summary:
The PR adds manual-run awareness to cron result application, records `lastRunWasManual`, updates gateway cron state schemas, and expands cron scheduler regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +65, Tests +851. Total +916 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main and `v2026.6.8` route manual cron completion through `applyJobResult` without a manual discriminator, so successful `deleteAfterRun` at-jobs can be removed and counters are updated. I did not run a live gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer policy acceptance for compatibility-sensitive cron scheduler state, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(cron): skip deleteAfterRun and preserve counters for manual runs (#83538)
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw cron run` (manual/debug runs) on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes the job on success — consuming the one-shot and preventing its scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters, interfering with scheduled retry/backoff state.

This PR adds an `isManual` flag to `applyJobResult` so manual runs preserve the job and leave scheduled execution state untouched.

## Root cause

`applyJobResult()` has no concept of manual vs scheduled runs. When a manual run succeeds on a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job, `shouldDelete` evaluates to `true` and the job is removed from the store — the scheduled fire never happens.

Additionally, manual error/skipped/success outcomes unconditionally mutate `consecutiveErrors` and `consecutiveSkipped` counters, which control retry backoff and auto-disable behavior for scheduled runs.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `timer.ts` | Add `isManual` option to `applyJobResult`; skip `deleteAfterRun` for manual runs; guard all counter mutations and alert emissions with `!isManual` |
| `ops.ts` | Pass `isManual: true` from manual run completion and invalid-persisted-run handling |
| `timer.regression.test.ts` | 5 new tests: manual ok/error preserve job, manual doesn't increment/reset counters |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts
 ✓ cron (45 tests) 144ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  45 passed (45)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes it, preventing scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate scheduled retry counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `aa92a4a5b0`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.

### Source-level proof

```
Main branch applyJobResult():
  - shouldDelete = kind === "at" && deleteAfterRun && status === "ok"
    → no manual discriminator, always deletes on success
  - consecutiveErrors incremented unconditionally on error
  - consecutiveSkipped incremented unconditionally on skip

Fix branch:
  - shouldDelete = wouldDelete && !opts?.isManual
    → manual runs never trigger deletion
  - Counter mutations guarded: if (!opts?.isManual) { ... }
    → manual runs don't affect retry/backoff state
```

### Regression test coverage

```
✅ manual ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted
✅ manual error on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted  
✅ scheduled ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job deleted (unchanged behavior)
✅ manual error does not increment consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
✅ manual success does not reset consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
```

- **What was not tested**: Live `openclaw cron run` CLI flow (requires a running gateway with a configured `deleteAfterRun` at-job). The `isManual` flag propagation from `ops.ts` to `applyJobResult` is validated at the unit level.

Closes #83538


### Re-verification (2026-05-20)

Local checkout at `d89f14de26`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

 ✓ cron  ../../src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (39 tests) 116ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  39 passed (39)
   Duration  152ms
```

Full cron suite (`src/cron/`) also run: 94 test files, 901 passed. The 7 failures are in unrelated files (`run-log.test.ts`, `service.failure-alert.test.ts`) pre-existing on upstream/main.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `openclaw cron run` (manual) of a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job incorrectly deletes the job. Manual runs also pollute `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `0cec2acc7a`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On `upstream/main`, counter mutations and `shouldDelete` run unconditionally — no `isManual` discriminator exists.
- **Exact steps after patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` + `src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: 46/46 timer regression tests pass (including 7 new #83538/#83933-specific tests). 7/7 failure-alert tests pass.
- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI flow; the tests exercise the same `applyJobResult` code path the Gateway calls internally.

### Key test results

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun + success | NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun + success | Deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |
| Manual error → failure alert | NOT triggered ✅ |
| Scheduled error → failure alert | Triggered ✅ |


## Real behavior proof (Docker, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker container, `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched source at `885d605e56`.

### Latest fixes

| Issue | Problem | Fix |
|-------|---------|-----|
| P1 | Manual at-job error was disabling job (`enabled=false`, `nextRunAtMs=undefined`) | At-job error retry/disable block now guarded with `if (opts?.isManual)` |
| P2 | Manual success was clearing `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown | Cooldown clearing now inside `if (!opts?.isManual)` guard |

### Test results

| Test Suite | Result |
|------------|--------|
| Timer regression (49 tests) | ✅ All passed |
| Failure-alert (7 tests) | ✅ All passed |

### Key verified behaviors

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual at-job error | `enabled` stays true, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| Manual success | `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown preserved ✅ |
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun | Job NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun | Job deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |


## Real CLI proof (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway at `885d605e56` running with `--allow-unconfigured`.

**Terminal transcript**: `openclaw cron add` → `cron show` → `cron run` (manual, triggers error) → `cron show` (job still exists, enabled=true, nextRunAtMs preserved) → `cron list` (job present) → `cron runs` (run history with error status, nextRunAtMs unchanged).

| Step | Command | Result |
|------|---------|--------|
| Add job | `openclaw cron add --name proof-job --at ... --delete-after-run` | Created with `deleteAfterRun: true` |
| Manual run | `openclaw cron run <id>` | `{"ok": true, "enqueued": true}` |
| After run | `openclaw cron show <id>` | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` ✅ |
| Job exists | `openclaw cron list` | Job still listed ✅ |
| Run history | `openclaw cron runs --id <id>` | `status: "error"`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` ✅ |


## Real CLI proof at head SHA `642c3450d9` (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway built from PR head.

| Test | Command | Before (main) | After (PR head) |
|------|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| A: at-job deleteAfterRun | `cron run` → `cron show` | Job deleted/disabled | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| B: every-job manual error | `cron run` → `cron show` | `nextRunAtMs` rewritten by backoff | `nextRunAtMs` unchanged ✅ |
| A+B: counters | `cron runs` | `consecutiveErrors` mutated | Unchanged ✅ |

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` no longer consumes `deleteAfterRun` one-shot jobs, and no longer perturbs retry counters, at-job retry/disable state, recurring backoff, or failure-alert cooldown.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container built from PR HEAD `3d32c9baff`, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.14.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `applyJobResult` has no manual-run discriminator — manual runs unconditionally drive `deleteAfterRun`, counter mutations, at-job disable, recurring backoff, and alert cooldown. The 12 regression tests added by this PR do not exist on main.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Build Docker image from PR HEAD
docker build --target build -t openclaw-pr83933:test .

# Test 1: Timer regression tests (includes all 12 manual-run isolation tests)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

# Test 2: Failure alert tests (adapted for manual-run isolation)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts

# Test 3: Lint check
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test npx oxlint src/cron/service/timer.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.ts

# Test 4: Full cron test suite
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Test 1 — Timer regression: **55 tests passed** (284ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (55 tests) 284ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  55 passed (55)
```

Test 2 — Failure alert: **10 tests passed** (63ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (10 tests) 63ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
```

Test 3 — Lint: **0 errors, 0 warnings**
```
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 13ms on 4 files with 189 rules using 10 threads.
```

Test 4 — Full cron suite: **1000 tests passed** across 98 test files
```
 Test Files  1 failed | 97 passed (98)
      Tests  2 failed | 1000 passed (1002)
```
The 2 failures are in `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts` — these look for Swift source files (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/CronModels.swift`) which are not present in the Docker build target. These failures are pre-existing and unrelated to this PR.

- **Observed result after fix**: All 12 new regression tests pass, confirming that manual runs preserve one-shot jobs (`deleteAfterRun`), do not increment/reset counters, do not disable at-jobs on error, do not rewrite recurring `nextRunAtMs` with backoff, and do not clear failure-alert cooldown. Control-group tests confirm scheduled runs still drive all 5 state effects normally.

- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI `openclaw cron run` end-to-end (covered by earlier Docker CLI proof at `642c3450d9` — subsequent commits were formatting-only changes via oxfmt). The `cron-protocol-conformance` tests require macOS app source and cannot run in Docker.


## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `6bc3b9e374`, 2026-05-30)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` no longer consumes `deleteAfterRun` one-shot jobs, and no longer perturbs retry counters, at-job retry/disable state, recurring backoff, failure-alert cooldown, or skipped-run alert counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container built from PR HEAD `6bc3b9e374`, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.14.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `applyJobResult` has no manual-run discriminator — manual runs unconditionally drive all 5 scheduled-state effects plus skipped-run alert paths. The 14 regression tests added by this PR do not exist on main.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Build Docker image from PR HEAD
docker build --target build -t openclaw-pr83933:test .

# Test 1: Timer regression tests (all 14 manual-run isolation tests)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

# Test 2: Failure alert tests (adapted for manual-run isolation)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts

# Test 3: Lint check
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test npx oxlint src/cron/service/timer.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.ts

# Test 4: Full cron test suite
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Test 1 — Timer regression: **56 tests passed** (234ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (56 tests) 234ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  56 passed (56)
```

Test 2 — Failure alert: **11 tests passed** (92ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (11 tests) 92ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)
```

Test 3 — Lint: **0 errors, 0 warnings**
```
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 10ms on 4 files with 189 rules using 10 threads.
```

Test 4 — Full cron suite: **1002 tests passed** across 98 test files
```
 Test Files  1 failed | 97 passed (98)
      Tests  2 failed | 1002 passed (1004)
```
The 2 failures are in `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts` — these look for Swift source files (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/CronModels.swift`) which are not present in the Docker build target. Pre-existing and unrelated to this PR.

- **Observed result after fix**: All 14 new regression tests pass, covering all 6 guarded state effects including the newly-added manual skipped-run isolation:
  - Manual `deleteAfterRun` at-job → NOT deleted ✅
  - Manual error → counters NOT incremented ✅
  - Manual success → counters NOT reset, cooldown NOT cleared ✅
  - Manual at-job error → NOT disabled, nextRunAtMs preserved ✅
  - Manual every-job error → backoff NOT applied, nextRunAtMs preserved ✅
  - Manual skipped at-job → consecutiveSkipped NOT incremented, alert NOT fired, all state preserved ✅
  - Manual skipped run with `includeSkipped: true` → failure alert NOT emitted ✅

  Control-group tests confirm scheduled runs still drive all state effects normally.

- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI `openclaw cron run` end-to-end (covered by earlier Docker CLI proof at `642c3450d9` — subsequent commits were formatting + test coverage changes). The `cron-protocol-conformance` tests require macOS app source and cannot run in Docker.

### Manual-run state contract

`cron run` is a debug/verification command. Manual runs record their outcome (lastRunAtMs, lastStatus, lastError) but do not advance scheduled state. This makes manual runs safe, non-destructive operations that cannot interfere with scheduled execution. No reasonable user scenario depends on manual runs consuming one-shot jobs or driving retry/backoff state.

## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `ccb89e3f11`, 2026-06-02)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `lastRunWasManual` field was missing from the `CronJobStateSchema` and `CronJobStatePatchSchema` in `packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/cron.ts`. Both schemas use `additionalProperties: false`, so returning a cron job state containing `lastRunWasManual` would fail gateway protocol validation on `cron list`/`cron show`. Added `lastRunWasManual: Type.Optional(Type.Boolean())` to both schemas.

- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`), Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.16.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`, patched OpenClaw at commit `ccb89e3f11`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: On the previous commit (`45efd1abdd`), `CronJobStateSchema` did not include `lastRunWasManual`. Any cron job state object with `lastRunWasManual: true` (set by `timer.ts:414` after a manual run) would be rejected by TypeBox validation due to `additionalProperties: false`. The `CronJobStatePatchSchema` was similarly missing the field, preventing state patches that include it.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
docker run --rm \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" \
  -v /app/node_modules \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" \
  node:24-bookworm \
  bash -c '
    cd /app && npm install 2>&1 | tail -3 && \
    node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
      src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts \
      src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts \
      src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts \
      src/cron/cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts \
      src/cron/cron-protocol-schema.test.ts 2>&1'
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (56 tests) 335ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts (15 tests) 54ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (10 tests) 31ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts (3 tests) 3ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/cron-protocol-schema.test.ts (2 tests) 0ms

 Test Files  5 passed (5)
      Tests  86 passed (86)
   Start at  07:37:28
   Duration  3.44s (transform 2.31s, setup 280ms, import 2.62s, tests 424ms, environment 0ms)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: All 86 tests pass including:
  - `timer.regression.test.ts`: 56 tests — manual run sets `lastRunWasManual: true` on state (line 2531), state now passes schema validation.
  - `service.restart-catchup.test.ts`: 15 tests — restart catch-up checks `lastRunWasManual` in state fixture (line 224) and correctly re-runs the job.
  - `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts`: 3 tests — schema conformance verified (delivery modes, status shape, failover reasons).
  - `cron-protocol-schema.test.ts`: 2 tests — schema structure validated (deprecated alias, failure notification state).

- **What was not tested**: Live `cron list`/`cron show` gateway roundtrip with the patched schema. The schema fix is structural (adding a field to a TypeBox object with `additionalProperties: false`) and the test suite validates that state objects with `lastRunWasManual` are accepted by the schema.

## Live Gateway CLI roundtrip proof (Docker, HEAD `ccb89e3f11`)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job must not delete the job, must not disable it, must not clear `nextRunAtMs`, and must set `lastRunWasManual: true` in state. The `lastRunWasManual` field must pass `CronJobStateSchema` validation (which uses `additionalProperties: false`).
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (linux/arm64, OrbStack), Node.js `v24.16.0`, OpenClaw Gateway built from source at commit `ccb89e3f11`.
- **Steps**:
  1. Built OpenClaw from source on the PR branch
  2. Started Gateway inside Docker with `gateway run --dev --allow-unconfigured --auth token`
  3. Created a `deleteAfterRun` at-job via `cron add`
  4. Manually ran the job via `cron run` (resulted in error due to missing codex-linux-arm64 in Docker — this is the interesting case: manual error on a deleteAfterRun job)
  5. Verified job survival, `lastRunWasManual: true`, `enabled: true`, and `nextRunAtMs` preserved via `cron show` and `cron list`
  6. Verified `cron list` and `cron show` returned the `lastRunWasManual` field without schema validation errors

<details>
<summary>Terminal output (full Docker CLI roundtrip)</summary>

```
=== Environment ===
Node: v24.16.0
OS: Linux 6.19.13-orbstack-gbd1dc07b8cf4 aarch64
Arch: aarch64
Container: node:24-bookworm (linux/arm64)
Commit: ccb89e3f11

=== Starting Gateway ===
Gateway ready after 3s

=== Step 1: cron add (deleteAfterRun at-job) ===
{
  "id": "d8ceb482-1159-4c0d-b609-f90cd0297e01",
  "name": "proof-delete-after-run",
  "enabled": true,
  "deleteAfterRun": true,
  "createdAtMs": 1780389603345,
  "schedule": {
    "kind": "at",
    "at": "2026-12-31T15:59:59.000Z"
  },
  "sessionTarget": "isolated",
  "state": {
    "nextRunAtMs": 1798732799000
  }
}

=== Step 2: cron list (before manual run) ===
{
  "jobs": [
    {
      "id": "d8ceb482-1159-4c0d-b609-f90cd0297e01",
      "name": "proof-delete-after-run",
      "enabled": true,
      "deleteAfterRun": true,
      "state": { "nextRunAtMs": 1798732799000 },
      "status": "idle"
    }
  ],
  "total": 1
}

=== Step 3: cron show (before manual run) ===
{
  "id": "d8ceb482-1159-4c0d-b609-f90cd0297e01",
  "enabled": true,
  "deleteAfterRun": true,
  "state": { "nextRunAtMs": 1798732799000 },
  "status": "idle"
}

=== Step 4: cron run (manual execution) ===
{
  "ok": true,
  "enqueued": true,
  "runId": "manual:d8ceb482-1159-4c0d-b609-f90cd0297e01:1780389607244:1"
}

=== Step 5: cron show (AFTER manual run) ===
{
  "id": "d8ceb482-1159-4c0d-b609-f90cd0297e01",
  "name": "proof-delete-after-run",
  "enabled": true,                          ← NOT disabled by manual error
  "deleteAfterRun": true,                   ← still set, not consumed
  "state": {
    "nextRunAtMs": 1798732799000,           ← preserved, not cleared
    "lastRunAtMs": 1780389607245,
    "lastRunStatus": "error",
    "lastRunWasManual": true,               ← NEW FIELD: schema accepted it
    "lastStatus": "error",
    "lastDurationMs": 15835
  },
  "status": "error"
}

=== Step 6: cron list (AFTER manual run - verify job still exists) ===
{
  "jobs": [
    {
      "id": "d8ceb482-1159-4c0d-b609-f90cd0297e01",
      "name": "proof-delete-after-run",
      "enabled": true,
      "deleteAfterRun": true,
      "state": {
        "nextRunAtMs": 1798732799000,
        "lastRunStatus": "error",
        "lastRunWasManual": true,
        "lastStatus": "error"
      },
      "status": "error"
    }
  ],
  "total": 1
}

=== Step 7: cron runs (run history) ===
{
  "entries": [
    {
      "jobId": "d8ceb482-1159-4c0d-b609-f90cd0297e01",
      "action": "finished",
      "status": "error",
      "runId": "manual:d8ceb482-1159-4c0d-b609-f90cd0297e01:1780389607244:1",
      "runAtMs": 1780389607245,
      "durationMs": 15835,
      "nextRunAtMs": 1798732799000,
      "jobName": "proof-delete-after-run"
    }
  ],
  "total": 1
}

Gateway stopped.
=== PROOF COMPLETE ===
```

</details>

- **Observed result**: After manual `cron run` (which triggered an error due to missing codex dependency in Docker):
  - Job **survived** — still present in `cron list` and `cron show` ✅
  - `enabled: true` — manual error did NOT disable the at-job ✅
  - `nextRunAtMs: 1798732799000` — preserved, NOT cleared ✅
  - `deleteAfterRun: true` — still set, NOT consumed by manual run ✅
  - `lastRunWasManual: true` — new field present and accepted by schema ✅
  - No `consecutiveErrors` field — manual error did NOT increment counter ✅
  - Run history shows `runId: "manual:..."` prefix confirming manual execution path ✅
  - Both `cron show` and `cron list` returned `lastRunWasManual` without schema validation errors, proving `CronJobStateSchema` correctly includes the field ✅

- **What was not tested**: Manual run with `status: "ok"` (would require a working LLM provider in Docker). The `shouldDelete` guard path (`wouldDelete && !opts?.isManual`) is exercised by unit tests. Multi-node cluster, production load.


## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `8596607fca`, 2026-06-02 — backoff fix)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a recurring job that errors must not open a backoff window that blocks the next scheduled fire. Previously, `isErrorBackoffPending()`, `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()`, `isPendingErrorBackoffSlot()`, and `recomputeJobNextRunAtMs()` read `lastRunStatus` and `lastRunAtMs` without checking `lastRunWasManual`, causing a manual error to push `nextRunAtMs` forward and block the timer-scheduled fire.

- **Real environment tested**: Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (linux/arm64), Node.js `v24.16.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`, patched OpenClaw at commit `8596607fca`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` does not check `lastRunWasManual`. A recurring every-job with `lastRunWasManual: true` and `lastRunStatus: "error"` would have `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` return a backoff deadline, `isErrorBackoffPending()` would return `true`, and `isRunnableJob()` would return `false` — blocking the next scheduled fire. `recomputeJobNextRunAtMs()` would also push `nextRunAtMs` forward to the backoff floor. Test file authored as part of this PR.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Unit tests in Docker
docker run --rm --platform linux/arm64 \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" -e CI=true \
  node:24-bookworm bash -c \
  "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts \
    src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts \
    src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts"

# Live Gateway CLI in Docker
docker run --rm --platform linux/arm64 \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" -e CI=true \
  -e OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-test \
  -e OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=test-token-123 \
  node:24-bookworm bash -c '
    mkdir -p /tmp/openclaw-test
    echo "{\"gateway\":{\"port\":19001,\"bind\":\"loopback\"}}" > /tmp/openclaw-test/openclaw.json
    node openclaw.mjs gateway run --dev --allow-unconfigured --token test-token-123 --port 19001 &
    sleep 8
    node openclaw.mjs cron add --name test-at-delete --at "2026-06-02T23:00:00Z" --message reminder --delete-after-run --url ws://127.0.0.1:19001 --token test-token-123
    # extract ID, then: cron run <ID>, cron show <ID>, cron list
    node openclaw.mjs cron add --name test-recurring --every 10s --message "recurring check" --url ws://127.0.0.1:19001 --token test-token-123
    # extract ID, then: cron run <ID>, cron show <ID>
    kill %1'
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

<details>
<summary>Docker unit test output (82 tests pass)</summary>

```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (57 tests) 254ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts (15 tests) 44ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (10 tests) 25ms

 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  82 passed (82)
   Start at  11:08:37
   Duration  2.77s (transform 1.83s, setup 250ms, import 2.09s, tests 323ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 5.85s
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>Docker Gateway CLI output</summary>

```
=== Node.js version ===
v24.16.0
=== Starting Gateway ===
[gateway] ready

TEST 1: Create deleteAfterRun at-job
  → id: dbfbe16b-c44a-4aa1-b820-889dbdbcb9a2, deleteAfterRun: true, nextRunAtMs: 1780441200000

TEST 2: Manual run on deleteAfterRun at-job
  → { "ok": true, "enqueued": true, "runId": "manual:dbfbe16b-..." }

TEST 3: Verify at-job survived manual run
  → id: dbfbe16b-..., enabled: true, deleteAfterRun: true, nextRunAtMs: 1780441200000 (PRESERVED)
  → status: "running" (agent executing but will error — no LLM provider in Docker)

TEST 4: Create recurring every-10s job
  → id: 18b2583a-7990-4667-95cd-591614c858f9, everyMs: 10000, nextRunAtMs: 1780398505454

TEST 5: Manual run on recurring job
  → { "ok": true, "enqueued": true, "runId": "manual:18b2583a-..." }

TEST 6: Verify recurring job state after manual run
  → id: 18b2583a-..., enabled: true, nextRunAtMs: 1780398505454 (PRESERVED, NOT pushed by backoff)

TEST 7: Schema validation — cron list --json
  → Both jobs returned with valid schema, no validation errors
  → total: 2, both enabled, both with preserved nextRunAtMs

Gateway stopped.
```

</details>

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. **deleteAfterRun at-job manual run**: Job survived manual run, `enabled: true`, `deleteAfterRun: true`, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅
  2. **Recurring job manual run**: Job's `nextRunAtMs` preserved at natural cadence, NOT pushed forward by backoff ✅
  3. **Schema validation**: Both `cron list --json` and `cron show --json` returned `lastRunWasManual` field without validation errors ✅
  4. **New regression test**: `#83933: manual error on recurring job does not block scheduled fire via backoff` — verifies `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` returns `undefined` for manual errors, `isRunnableJob()` returns `true` within theoretical backoff window, `recomputeNextRunsForMaintenance()` computes natural cadence (not backoff floor), and control case confirms scheduled errors still apply backoff ✅

- **What was not tested**: Manual run with `status: "ok"` on recurring job (requires working LLM in Docker). Multi-node cluster, production load, actual delivery to chat channels.

target.title: fix(gateway): preserve text from oversized multimodal history
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve recoverable text blocks from oversized multimodal transcript lines
- replace omitted large image payloads with a lightweight placeholder instead of dropping the full message
- add a regression test covering text + oversized image history replay

Fixes #78729

## Root cause
When a transcript JSONL line exceeded the oversized-line guard, the tail reader fell back to a generic placeholder record and replaced the entire message with `[chat.history omitted: message too large]`. That meant a large inline image/base64 payload could cause nearby normal text in the same multimodal message to disappear from `chat.history`.

## Testing
- `pnpm test -- src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts`

target.title: fix(agents): block raw self-update package installs
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: agent self-update requests could run raw global package-manager upgrades such as `sudo npm install -g openclaw@latest`, replacing package files while the old Gateway process kept running.
- Why it matters: stale running gateways can later lazy-import removed hashed runtime chunks, producing `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` or silent channel hangs.
- What changed: the exec tool now blocks raw global package-manager installs/updates of the `openclaw` package and points operators toward `update.run` / `openclaw update`, which owns restart handling.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): normal `openclaw update` is still allowed, local non-global `npm install openclaw` is not blocked, and non-OpenClaw packages are unaffected.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #64892
- Related #24085, #60439
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: raw `npm install -g openclaw@latest` style self-updates are rejected by `exec` before process launch.
- Real environment tested: local Ubuntu workspace, Node 24.15.0, pnpm 10.33.2.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/agents/bash-tools.exec.script-preflight.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): targeted regression test passed with 59 passed / 4 skipped.
- Observed result after fix: direct, sudo-wrapped, shell-wrapped, and `env -S` raw OpenClaw package upgrades are rejected; `openclaw update`, local `npm install openclaw`, and `@openclaw/sdk` global installs remain unblocked by this guard.
- What was not tested: a live service-managed Gateway self-update flow.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): #64892 includes journal evidence of `sudo npm install -g openclaw@latest` followed by stale chunk import failures.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the system prompt told agents to use `update.run`, but the exec surface did not enforce that guidance and still allowed raw global OpenClaw package-manager upgrades.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no exec-side parser blocked global `npm`/`pnpm`/`bun`/`yarn` installs or updates of the `openclaw` package.
- Contributing context (if known): package-manager upgrades can replace `dist/` while a service-managed Gateway keeps the old process image alive.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.script-preflight.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: exec rejects raw global OpenClaw package-manager upgrades, including common wrappers, without blocking `openclaw update` or unrelated package installs.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is command-selection enforcement at the exec boundary, so a focused parser/tool test covers the behavior before any host command runs.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing channel-login guard tests covered the same exec-control-command pattern but not self-update package-manager installs.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Agents can no longer use `exec` to run raw global package-manager upgrades of the `openclaw` package. They must use `update.run` or `openclaw update` for self-updates.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[user asks self-update] -> exec: npm install -g openclaw@latest -> package files replaced -> old gateway imports missing chunks

After:
[user asks self-update] -> exec rejects raw package-manager upgrade -> agent uses update.run/openclaw update -> supported restart path
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this narrows command execution by blocking one unsafe self-update class and directing users to the supported update path.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu
- Runtime/container: Node 24.15.0, pnpm 10.33.2
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): exec tool / agent self-update
- Relevant config (redacted): default local test config

### Steps

1. Create an exec tool with gateway host execution.
2. Attempt `sudo npm install -g openclaw@latest`.
3. Attempt wrapped variants such as `sudo -u openclaw bash -lc 'pnpm add --global openclaw@latest'` and `env -S 'npm install -g openclaw@latest'`.

### Expected

- The exec tool rejects the raw package-manager OpenClaw upgrade before launch and tells the agent to use `update.run` / `openclaw update`.

### Actual

- Matches expected in the added regression tests.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: raw npm/pnpm/bun/yarn global OpenClaw package upgrades are detected; sudo/shell/env wrappers are rejected; allowed non-target commands stay allowed by the parser.
- Edge cases checked: `openclaw update`, local `npm install openclaw`, and `npm install -g @openclaw/sdk` are not classified as raw OpenClaw self-upgrades.
- What you did **not** verify: live service-managed Gateway update/restart behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a legitimate operator expected the agent to run raw `npm install -g openclaw` through exec.
  - Mitigation: the error message points to `update.run` / `openclaw update`, the supported path that restarts the gateway safely.

target.title: feat: fire session reset hooks for daily and idle resets
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Internal hooks and `before_reset` plugin hook only fire for manual `/new` and `/reset` commands, not for daily (4AM) or idle-timeout session resets. This means `session-memory` never saves on daily/idle resets.
- Why it matters: 4 community issues report lost session memory on daily/idle resets (#10142, #31266, #50891, #43524)
- What changed: Wire both hook types into the lazy staleness path in `initSessionState()`, update `session-memory` registration and filter
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No new hook event names, no changes to plugin SDK API, no extraction/refactor — only `export` added to existing functions

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Memory / storage

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #10142
- Closes #31266
- Closes #50891
- Closes #43524
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `initSessionState()` fires `session_end`/`session_start` plugin hooks for lazy resets but never fires `triggerInternalHook` or `hookRunner.runBeforeReset`. These only fire via `emitResetCommandHooks()` in `commands-core.ts`, which is only called for manual `/new` and `/reset` commands.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test asserted that internal hooks fire for lazy resets
- Contributing context (if known): The lazy evaluation design means sessions aren't proactively killed — staleness is checked on next message arrival, a code path separate from manual commands

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/session.stale-hooks.test.ts` (new), `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts` (extended)
- Scenario the test should lock in: Internal hook fires with `action: "daily"` / `"idle"` when session is stale; `before_reset` plugin hook fires for lazy resets; no double-fire on manual reset
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Mocks `triggerInternalHook` and `hookRunner` to assert dispatch without side-effects
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `session-hooks-context.test.ts` covers `session_end`/`session_start` but not internal hooks or `before_reset`

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`session-memory` now saves session summaries on daily (4AM) and idle-timeout resets, not just manual `/new` and `/reset`. Plugin `before_reset` hook now fires for lazy resets, giving plugins pre-archive transcript access.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[lazy reset] -> session_end + session_start hooks only

After:
[lazy reset] -> internal hook (command:daily/idle) + before_reset + session_end + session_start
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Any
- Runtime/container: Node.js 24
- Integration/channel (if any): Any channel with daily or idle reset configured

### Steps

1. Configure a session with daily reset (default 4AM)
2. Have an active session with messages before 4AM
3. Send a message after 4AM (triggering lazy staleness detection)

### Expected

- `session-memory` saves a summary of the previous session
- `before_reset` plugin hook fires with `reason: "daily"` and transcript messages

### Actual (before this PR)

- `session-memory` does not fire — no summary saved
- `before_reset` plugin hook does not fire

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Daily reset with stale session triggers session-memory save; idle reset triggers save; manual /new does not double-fire
- Edge cases checked: First-ever session (no previous entry), system events (heartbeat), fresh session
- What you did **not** verify: End-to-end with real LLM slug generation (mocked in tests)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `before_reset` receives the post-archive `sessionFile` path (after `renameSync`), not the original path. `loadBeforeResetTranscript` handles ENOENT by scanning for the latest `.reset.*` archived sibling.
  - Mitigation: This matches the manual reset path behavior. The stable post-archive path is intentional — it avoids racing transcript reads against the archive rename.

## AI Disclosure

- [x] Mark as AI-assisted in the PR title or description
- [x] Note the degree of testing: fully tested — 10 unit tests (7 new + 3 extended) + manual daily/idle reset verification
- [x] Confirm: I understand what this code does and have verified the hook dispatch logic, guard conditions, and fire-and-forget patterns

🤖 AI-assisted with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: feat(discord): add event-edit and event-delete actions
target.body:
## Summary - Problem: agents could create Discord scheduled events through the message tool but had no way to update or cancel them, so descriptions, locations, times, and cover art went stale until a human edited them in Discord - Why it matters: rounds out the scheduled-event lifecycle so agents (e.g. a Listening Party host swapping in a featured album) can keep event metadata in sync without human intervention - What changed: added `event-edit` and `event-delete` message-tool actions, gated by the existing `events` action gate, with typed PATCH/DELETE REST helpers, partial-PATCH runtime that only sends fields the agent provided, CLI subcommands, docs, changelog, and tests - What did NOT change (scope boundary): no new core seam, no changes to other channels, no changes to `event-list` / `event-create`, no fetch-then-merge round trip (Discord PATCH already only changes fields present in the body) ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [x] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes #66791 - Related #60883 (prior `event-create` cover-image work that this builds on) - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: agents could not update or delete Discord scheduled events through the message tool; only `event-list` and `event-create` were exposed - Real environment tested: local OpenClaw built from this branch (`pnpm install && pnpm build`) against a real Discord guild I admin, using a bot installed with `Manage Events`; plus the typed REST fake suite for the wire-shape proof - Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. `pnpm openclaw message event create --guild-id --event-name "Listening Party" --start-time 2026-05-15T19:00:00Z --end-time 2026-05-15T21:00:00Z --event-type external --location "https://meet.example.com/listen" --desc "first session"` -> noted the returned event id 2. `pnpm openclaw message event edit --guild-id --event-id --desc "now featuring Album X"` -> only the description field was sent in the PATCH 3. Opened the event in Discord to confirm only the description changed; title, start/end time, and location were unchanged 4. `pnpm openclaw message event delete --guild-id --event-id ` 5. `pnpm openclaw message event list --guild-id ` to confirm the event is gone 6. Targeted Vitest suites: `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/internal/api.test.ts`, `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.test.ts`, `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts`, `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts`, `pnpm test src/infra/outbound/message-action-spec.test.ts`, `pnpm test src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`, `pnpm test src/agents/tool-display.test.ts` 7. `pnpm prompt:snapshots:gen` (regenerated codex prompt-snapshot fixtures for the new `eventId` schema field) 8. `pnpm check:changed` (typecheck + lint + import-cycles + boundary guards) - Evidence after fix: live Discord guild walked through create -> partial-edit -> delete cleanly (screenshot attached); targeted suites all green (8 + 53 + 12 + 21 + 24 + 43 + 21 tests); `pnpm check:changed` reports `Found 0 warnings and 0 errors` for all three oxlint shards and a green typecheck across all tsgo lanes - Observed result after fix: in Discord, the edit step changed only the description and left every other field byte-for-byte unchanged; the delete step removed the event from the guild's scheduled-events list. At the wire level, `event-edit` sends a partial PATCH with only the fields the agent provided and `event-delete` issues `DELETE /guilds/{guild.id}/scheduled-events/{event.id}`, both under the existing `events` action gate - What was not tested: stage / voice entity-type variants (only the `external` variant was exercised end-to-end on a real guild; the stage/voice mapping is covered by the new runtime tests) - Before evidence (optional): on `main`, `gh search code 'event-edit OR event-delete'` returns no hits in the Discord plugin or message-action surfaces ## Root Cause (if applicable) N/A (feature addition, not a regression) ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) N/A (new feature; coverage added in the PR is the smallest reliable guardrail) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `extensions/discord/src/internal/api.test.ts` (REST shape), `extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.test.ts` (eventEdit/eventDelete partial-PATCH and gating), `extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts` (events-gate exposes the full lifecycle) - Scenario the test should lock in: PATCH/DELETE route shape, partial-field forwarding, `entity_metadata.location` encoding when `eventType` is external, `events` gate enforcement - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: covers each of the action layers (REST helper, runtime, discovery) at the seam an agent would actually exercise, without paying for a live Discord round trip - Existing test that already covers this: none for edit/delete; `event-create` had no analogous handle-action/runtime test before this PR either - If no new test is added, why not: n/a, tests added ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - New message-tool actions on Discord: `event-edit` (required `guildId`+`eventId`, optional `eventName`/`desc`/`location`/`startTime`/`endTime`/`eventType`/`channelId`/`image`) and `event-delete` (required `guildId`+`eventId`) - New CLI subcommands: `pnpm openclaw message event edit ...` and `pnpm openclaw message event delete ...` - Both actions stay behind the existing `channels.discord.actions.events` gate, so existing deployments that disable that gate stay unchanged ## Diagram (if applicable) ```text Before: event-list / event-create -> Discord (GET / POST scheduled-events) [no edit / delete path; metadata goes stale until a human fixes it] After: event-list -> GET /guilds/{g}/scheduled-events event-create -> POST /guilds/{g}/scheduled-events event-edit -> PATCH /guilds/{g}/scheduled-events/{e} (only fields the agent provided) event-delete -> DELETE /guilds/{g}/scheduled-events/{e} ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? No (rides the existing `events` action gate) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - New/changed network calls? Yes; two new outbound calls to Discord's existing `guild scheduled-events` endpoint (PATCH and DELETE), reusing the same authenticated `RequestClient` already used by `event-list` / `event-create` - Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes; two new message-tool actions added to the `events` gate - Data access scope changed? No - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: deletion is destructive, so it stays gated by the same `events` flag that already controls create; partial-PATCH avoids accidentally clearing fields the agent did not mention ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Linux (WSL2) - Runtime/container: Node 22 + pnpm; locally built from this branch and run against a real Discord guild I admin (also covered at the wire level by the typed Discord REST fake suite) - Model/provider: n/a (action-surface change) - Integration/channel: Discord (`channels.discord`); bot installed in the test guild with the `Manage Events` permission - Relevant config (redacted): account with `channels.discord.actions.events: true` ### Steps 1. Configure a Discord account with the `events` gate enabled 2. `pnpm openclaw message event create --guild-id --event-name "Listening Party" --start-time ` and note the returned event id 3. `pnpm openclaw message event edit --guild-id --event-id --desc "now featuring Album X"` 4. `pnpm openclaw message event delete --guild-id --event-id ` ### Expected - After step 3 only the description changes; cover image, status, recurrence, and other fields stay untouched - After step 4 the event is gone from `pnpm openclaw message event list --guild-id ` ### Actual - Live Discord guild: `event create` produced a scheduled event with the listed metadata; `event edit --desc "now featuring Album X"` updated only the description in the Discord UI and left every other field unchanged; `event delete` removed the event and the follow-up `event list` returned an empty array. Screenshot of the Discord event card before and after the edit (and after the delete) attached to the PR - Wire level: partial PATCH body matches exactly the fields the agent passed (covered by `runtime.test.ts`); DELETE issues against `Routes.guildScheduledEvent("g1", "e1")` (covered by `internal/api.test.ts`) ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (channel-actions discovery test now asserts the full lifecycle; api.test.ts now asserts PATCH/DELETE shapes) - [x] Trace/log snippets (test command output above) - [x] Screenshot/recording (Discord event card after `event create`, after `event edit --desc`, and the empty `event list` after `event delete`) - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)
Event created
Event deleted
## Human Verification (required) - Verified scenarios: live Discord guild walkthrough of `event create` -> `event edit --desc` -> `event delete` -> `event list`, confirming via the Discord UI that the partial edit changed only the description and the delete removed the event; targeted Vitest suites listed above; `pnpm check:changed` (typecheck + lint + import-cycles + boundary); `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` on all 18 touched files - Edge cases checked: events gate disabled (rejects with `Discord events are disabled`); `eventType: "external"` with `location` encodes `entity_metadata.location`; omitted fields are not sent in the PATCH body; `eventDelete` returns `{ ok: true, eventId }` - What I did not verify on a live guild: `eventType: "stage"` and `eventType: "voice"` variants (only the `external` variant was exercised end-to-end; the other two are covered by the new runtime tests) ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? Yes - Config/env changes? No (uses existing `channels.discord.actions.events` gate) - Migration needed? No ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: agent passes `eventType` change without the dependent fields Discord requires (e.g. switching to `external` without `location` or `endTime`) - Mitigation: Discord's PATCH endpoint already enforces entity-type-specific constraints server-side; the runtime forwards the error to the agent; documented in `docs/channels/discord.md` - Risk: `event-delete` is destructive - Mitigation: rides the same `events` action gate as `event-create`, so anyone who already restricted event creation continues to restrict deletion ## AI assistance This PR was prepared with the help of an AI coding agent (Claude). The diff was authored against the existing `event-create` plumbing, every change was locally typechecked, linted, formatted, and covered by Vitest, and prompt snapshot fixtures were regenerated through `pnpm prompt:snapshots:gen`. Functionality was tested manually by me (human 🤡). ## Test plan - `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/internal/api.test.ts` - `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.test.ts` - `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts` - `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts` - `pnpm test src/infra/outbound/message-action-spec.test.ts` - `pnpm test src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts` - `pnpm test src/agents/tool-display.test.ts` - `pnpm prompt:snapshots:gen` - `pnpm check:changed` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ` ## Before / After Before: the Discord message tool exposed `event-list` and `event-create` only. Once an event was live, agents could not update its description, location, time window, cover image, or entity-type metadata, and could not delete the event. The CLI mirrored that gap with only `message event list` and `message event create` subcommands. After: agents can call `event-edit` (partial PATCH; only the fields the agent provides are sent, so unrelated state stays untouched) and `event-delete` through the message tool, and the CLI exposes `message event edit` / `message event delete` subcommands. Both new actions sit behind the existing `events` action gate. Discord's `PATCH` endpoint keeps enforcing entity-type-specific constraints server-side, so the runtime does not need a fetch-then-merge round trip.

target.title: test(plugins): cover stale OpenClaw peer repair during install
target.body:
## Summary

This adds an e2e regression for the dirty managed-npm state that can make `openclaw plugins install @opik/opik-openclaw` fail with `ERESOLVE` after prior host-peer plugin installs.

The fixture mirrors the bad state from the live box:

- a Codex-like plugin already installed with `peerOptional openclaw@>=2026.5.5-beta.2`
- an Opik-like plugin being installed with `peer openclaw@>=2026.3.2`
- a stale root-level `node_modules/openclaw@2026.5.4` left in the managed npm root

The test first proves raw npm still fails with `ERESOLVE` in that state, then proves OpenClaw's managed installer repairs the stale root package, completes the install, and restores plugin-local `openclaw` peer symlinks for both plugins.

## RCA

The failure is not just a future peer-materialization problem. Once an older OpenClaw registry package has already been materialized at `~/.openclaw/npm/node_modules/openclaw`, npm treats it as the candidate host peer during later managed-root mutations. A later install can then fail before the requested plugin is installed because another installed plugin has a stricter host peer range than the stale root package satisfies.

Current `main` already has the behavior fix: managed plugin installs repair stale root `openclaw` state before npm mutates the root, use `--omit=peer`, and relink plugin-local host peers afterward. This PR locks the exact dirty-state transition down so we do not regress users who already have poisoned managed npm roots.

## Verification

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/plugins/install.npm-spec.e2e.test.ts`
- `pnpm test:serial src/plugins/install.npm-spec.e2e.test.ts` - 1 file / 5 tests passed
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 pnpm check:changed` - attempted through Blacksmith Testbox; first box shut down while queued, second stayed queued and was stopped after waiting. Targeted local e2e proof above passed.

target.title: fix(gateway): skip health-monitor restart for terminal WhatsApp disconnects
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: On multi-tenant gateways running ≥1 WhatsApp account, any account that receives a terminal disconnect (`loggedOut` 401, `connectionReplaced` 440) enters a perpetual restart loop — the health monitor calls `startChannel()` on every check interval, each attempt leaks a full Baileys WebSocket + Noise handshake, and over time this exhausts gateway heap (12.9 GB reported over 20 h on 12 accounts), saturates the event loop (~80% CPU), and degrades RPC latency for all tenants on the same gateway (~24 s p99 vs. <200 ms baseline). Recovery requires manual intervention.

- **Root Cause**: Two independent restart paths both lacked a terminal-disconnect guard, and the status surface also missed the signal. (1) `ChannelHealthSnapshot` had no `terminalDisconnect` field, so `evaluateChannelHealth` returned `"not-running"` for every stopped channel regardless of why it stopped, causing the health-monitor to unconditionally call `startChannel()`. (2) The `ChannelManager` task-exit recovery handler fires immediately when the channel task settles — before the health monitor is involved — and only checked `manuallyStopped`, so terminal exits still triggered the backoff-and-restart loop independently. (3) WhatsApp's custom `buildAccountSnapshot` handler forwarded `healthState` from the runtime snapshot but not `terminalDisconnect`, so `channels.status` recomputed `"not-running"` instead of `"terminal-disconnect"` for logged-out accounts.

- **Fix**: Add `terminalDisconnect?: boolean` to `ChannelHealthSnapshot` and the public `ChannelAccountSnapshot`. The WhatsApp status controller sets the flag to `true` in `markStopped()` when `healthState` is `"logged-out"` or `"conflict"` (the only two states that clear auth and require QR re-pairing), and resets it to `undefined` in `noteConnected()` on successful re-auth. Both restart paths now observe the flag: `evaluateChannelHealth` returns a new `"terminal-disconnect"` reason that the health monitor logs and skips; the `ChannelManager` task-exit handler returns early with an info-level log. The flag is also forwarded in WhatsApp's `buildAccountSnapshot` so `channels.status` reflects the correct terminal state. The flag is channel-agnostic: any future extension can set it on its own `ChannelAccountSnapshot` to opt in to the same no-restart behavior without modifying core.

- **What changed**:
  - `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` — added `terminalDisconnect?: boolean` to `ChannelAccountSnapshot`
  - `src/gateway/channel-health-policy.ts` — added field to `ChannelHealthSnapshot`; added `"terminal-disconnect"` reason; added guard before the `not-running` branch
  - `src/gateway/channel-health-monitor.ts` — added skip block for `terminal-disconnect` reason with an info-level log line
  - `src/gateway/server-channels.ts` — added `terminalDisconnect` guard in the task-exit recovery handler after the `manuallyStopped` check
  - `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/types.ts` — added `terminalDisconnect?: boolean` to `WebChannelStatus`
  - `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor-state.ts` — set flag in `markStopped()` for `"logged-out"` / `"conflict"`; clear in `noteConnected()`
  - `extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.ts` — forwarded `terminalDisconnect` from the runtime snapshot in the custom `buildAccountSnapshot` handler
  - `src/gateway/channel-health-policy.test.ts` — 3 new cases covering terminal-disconnect evaluation, no-flag fallthrough, and running-channel flag ignore
  - `src/gateway/channel-health-monitor.test.ts` — 2 new cases proving the monitor skips terminal-disconnect channels and still restarts non-terminal stopped channels
  - `src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts` — 1 new case proving the manager task-exit path does not auto-restart a terminal-disconnect channel
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/channels.status.test.ts` — 1 new case proving `channels.status` reports `"terminal-disconnect"` health state when the flag is set

- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - No changes to reconnect policy, cooldown config, or hourly restart cap
  - No changes to any other channel extension (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.)
  - `isManuallyStopped()` logic is untouched; manually stopped channels continue to be skipped by the existing guard that runs before health evaluation
  - `resolveChannelRestartReason` is not reachable for `terminal-disconnect` (monitor skips before calling it) and is not modified
  - No new public-facing config keys or gateway events in this change

---

### Reproduction

1. Run a gateway with ≥1 WhatsApp account:
   ```yaml
   channels:
     whatsapp:
       accounts:
         default:
           enabled: true
   ```
2. From the linked phone: Settings → Linked Devices → unlink the session.
3. Observe `gateway.log`:
   ```
   [health-monitor] [whatsapp:default] health-monitor: restarting (reason: stopped)
   ```
   firing every check interval indefinitely.
4. After this fix:
   ```
   [default] auto-restart skipped, terminal disconnect
   [health-monitor] [whatsapp:default] health-monitor: skipping restart, terminal disconnect
   ```
   fires with no `startChannel()` call, and `channels.status` reports `healthState: "terminal-disconnect"`.

---

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: A terminal-disconnect channel will no longer be auto-recovered by either restart path. If `terminalDisconnect` is accidentally set `true` on a channel that could have reconnected, that channel would stay stopped until manually restarted.
- **Mitigation**: The flag is set only for `"logged-out"` and `"conflict"` healthStates, which both clear stored auth credentials — the channel genuinely cannot reconnect without operator QR action. `noteConnected()` unconditionally clears the flag, so a successful re-auth restores normal behavior on all paths. Three policy unit tests, two monitor integration tests, one manager recovery test, and one `channels.status` regression test cover the boundary cases.

---

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

---

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway
- [x] WhatsApp plugin
- [x] Channel status types (additive, backward-compatible)
- [x] Tests

---

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #78419

target.title: fix: prevent memory flush failure from aborting user reply (#85645)
target.body:
## Summary

When `memoryFlush` fails during the reply pipeline (provider timeout, rate limit, network error), the visible error payload was treated as the final reply via `replyOperation.fail("run_failed", ...)`. This aborted the user's actual message. Since the session remained over threshold, every subsequent message triggered the same flush failure, creating a permanent death loop where no real replies could be delivered.

## Changes

**`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts`**
- Memory flush visible error payloads are now logged via `logVerbose()` and **not** delivered as the reply. The main reply run proceeds normally.
- On flush exhaustion (`memoryFlushFailureCount` crosses `MAX_FLUSH_FAILURES` during the current turn), the bloated session is automatically rotated via `resetReplyRunSession()`. The old transcript file is **preserved on disk** (`cleanupTranscripts: false`) — rotation alone breaks the death loop (the new session no longer hits the bloated transcript), and keeping the old JSONL leaves it available for forensics/recovery. The active session only replays recent turns regardless of this flag.
- Added an **opt-in, non-terminal degraded notice**: when `agents.defaults.compaction.notifyUser` is enabled, flush exhaustion surfaces a short heads-up to the user via the existing `onBlockReply` bypass (same mechanism as `sendCompactionNotice`). It **never** calls `replyOperation.fail()` and **never** aborts the reply. Default (`notifyUser=false`) keeps the silent log-and-continue behavior. No new config surface is added.
- The `resetSession` / `resetSessionAfterRoleOrderingConflict` helpers are hoisted earlier in the function so they are available before the memory flush phase. No behavioral change to their existing callers; the role-ordering reset path keeps its original `cleanupTranscripts: true`.

**`src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts`**
- `initSessionState()` now clears `memoryFlushFailureCount`, `memoryFlushLastFailedAt`, and `memoryFlushLastFailureError` when a session is reset (e.g., via `/new`), preventing stale failure state from carrying over.

**`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.ts`**
- `resetReplyRunSession()` now clears `compactionCount` (reset to 0) and all `memoryFlush*` fields, ensuring a cleanly rotated session has no lingering flush state.

## What is NOT changed

- `runMemoryFlushIfNeeded` behavior is unchanged — it still tracks failures, increments `memoryFlushFailureCount`, and emits lifecycle events (`memory_flush_failed`, `memory_flush_exhausted`).
- `fallbacksOverride: []` is preserved — flush operations still use a fixed model/provider with no fallback chain.
- `MAX_FLUSH_FAILURES = 3` threshold is unchanged.
- No new configuration key is introduced — the opt-in notice reuses the existing `compaction.notifyUser` gate.

## Tests

- **`agent-runner-direct-runtime-config.test.ts`**: Verifies flush errors are logged and the main reply continues; verifies session rotation on flush exhaustion now uses `cleanupTranscripts: false`; covers the opt-in notice (fires on exhaustion when `notifyUser=true`, stays silent on transient failure, and never fires when `notifyUser=false`) — in all cases the main reply still proceeds.
- **`agent-runner-session-reset.test.ts`**: Verifies `compactionCount` and `memoryFlush*` fields are cleared on reset; adds a test proving that with `cleanupTranscripts: false` the session still rotates to a new id while the old transcript file is **preserved** on disk.
- **`session.test.ts`**: Verifies `initSessionState()` clears all memory flush state fields, with both in-memory assertions and persisted session store file reads.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: When `memoryFlush` / auto-compaction fails permanently (provider timeout, rate limit, 5xx), current `main` routes the visible flush error into `replyOperation.fail("run_failed")` before the main reply run, aborting the user's real reply. Because the session stays over threshold, every later message hits the same failure — a permanent death loop with no real replies delivered. This PR logs the flush failure and continues the main reply, rotates the exhausted session while preserving the old transcript on disk (`cleanupTranscripts: false`), clears stale flush state on reset, and adds an opt-in non-terminal degraded notice gated by the existing `compaction.notifyUser`.
- **Real environment tested**: OpenClaw Gateway run directly from this PR's source at current head `5dc502d76f` (branch `fix/memory-flush-death-loop-85645`) via `tsx` (`node --import tsx src/index.ts gateway run`), on macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Isolated state dir, `auth: none`, Gateway port `14881`. A mock OpenAI-completions provider on loopback `:3999` returns HTTP `500` for compaction/flush requests (detected by summarization-prompt keywords) and HTTP `200` for normal chat — isolating the flush-failure path while the main reply provider stays healthy. Config: `softThresholdTokens: 800`, `contextWindow: 4096`, `maxTokens: 1024`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Start the mock provider: `node mock-provider/server.mjs` (loopback `:3999`, returns 500 on flush requests, 200 on normal chat).
  2. Start the Gateway from this PR's source at head `5dc502d76f`: `node --import tsx src/index.ts gateway --force --port 14881 --auth none run` with an isolated state dir.
  3. Drive 12 user messages over the WebSocket protocol: 6 to grow the session past the 800-token soft threshold, then 6 more to repeatedly trigger the (permanently failing) flush. Wait for each `agent.wait` completion.
- **Before evidence (current main)**: On current `main`, visible memory-flush error payloads are collected and passed to `replyOperation.fail("run_failed")` before the main reply run, so the user's real reply is aborted and subsequent messages repeat the failure (`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts` flush-error path on `main`).
- **Evidence after fix**: Redacted runtime logs and terminal console output copied from a live OpenClaw Gateway run (`node --import tsx src/index.ts gateway --force --port 14881 --auth none run`) at head `5dc502d76f`, with private data redacted.

  Mock provider — every flush request rejected with 500 (36 flush 500s, 1 normal 200):
  ```
  [req#1]  NORMAL REQUEST — returning success
  [req#2]  FLUSH REQUEST DETECTED — returning 500
  [req#3]  FLUSH REQUEST DETECTED — returning 500
  ...
  (36 flush requests total, all returned HTTP 500)
  ```

  Gateway — flush failures logged, session mapping preserved, run continues (not aborted):
  ```
  [agent/embedded] auto-compaction failed for mock/mock-model:
    Summarization failed: 500 Mock provider: simulated flush failure
  [agent/embedded] [context-overflow-recovery] exhausted provider overflow recovery
    for mock/mock-model; livenessState=blocked suggestedAction=reset_or_new
  Auto-compaction failed (...). Preserving existing session mapping for [SESSION].
  ```

  WebSocket client — all 12 messages received a reply (`status=ok`), none aborted:
  ```
  [MSG-1]      agent done: status=ok
  [MSG-2]      agent done: status=ok
  [MSG-3]      agent done: status=ok
  [MSG-4]      agent done: status=ok
  [MSG-5]      agent done: status=ok
  [MSG-6]      agent done: status=ok
  [TRIGGER-7]  agent done: status=ok
  [TRIGGER-8]  agent done: status=ok
  [TRIGGER-9]  agent done: status=ok
  [TRIGGER-10] agent done: status=ok
  [TRIGGER-11] agent done: status=ok
  [TRIGGER-12] agent done: status=ok
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: With the flush provider permanently broken (36 consecutive 500s), all 12 user messages still completed with `status=ok` — the death loop is gone. The flush failure is logged rather than delivered as a terminal `run_failed` reply, and the session mapping is preserved/rotated instead of aborting the turn. This is the exact condition that previously produced the permanent loop on `main`.
- **What was not tested**: Live third-party LLM providers (the flush failure is simulated with a deterministic mock 500 to make the path reproducible); long-horizon multi-session persistence across Gateway restarts; and the maintainer-judgment items (session-rotation liveness tradeoff and the opt-in notice semantics) which are product decisions rather than testable code behavior.

target.title: fix: heartbeat transcript uses effectiveBaseBody and metadata-aware filter
target.body:
## Summary

Fix heartbeat mechanism bug causing runtime-context drift and delayed responses.

### Issue 1: Metadata-prefixed heartbeat prompts not filtered
- heartbeat-filter.ts: strip inbound metadata before checking patterns
- heartbeat-filter.test.ts: add test for metadata-prefixed prompts

### Issue 2: Runtime-context when transcript marker not in effectivePrompt
- runtime-context-prompt.ts: when heartbeat transcript marker is not found in effectivePrompt, preserve compact marker as visible prompt and send full effective heartbeat body as runtimeContext. This preserves the transcript marker invariant while ensuring model receives full heartbeat content.

## Verification
pnpm test src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts -- --run

Fixes #78423
